entry.title: Application Choices and Decisions for The Contract Management Process
entry.content:
Summary

This article seeks to provide guidance on deciding the most suitable target application architecture to support Contract Management needs of an organization.

With SAP Ariba and S/4HANA both delivering solution capabilities around Contract Management, along with a few other applications, enterprises now have multiple options to choose from and need to make their selection by comparing their organization’s business capability requirements to the solution capabilities the different applications offer.

The licensing aspects of the components discussed should be considered during target architecture design and is beyond the scope of this evaluation.

Business Requirements

Purpose

Contract Management is carried out

  • to record terms and conditions agreed with a supplier, identified for a product or service through the Sourcing exercise, and
  • to ensure that every purchase of the said product or service within the validity period of such contract, happens from the contracted source

Basic Process

Input

  • Accepted Quotations at the conclusion of the Sourcing exercise provide all relevant inputs for Contracts
  • Business Roles  Purchaser/ Buyer, Sourcing Manager

Process Flow

  • Following information from the accepted Quotation is used to generate a Contract
    • supplier information like code, name, address, supply locations etc.
    • product or service information like code, description, unit of measure etc.
    • agreed terms like price, rebate, quantity limits, value limits, validity dates etc.
    • schedule information like quantity, delivery locations, dates etc.
  • Upon creation, Contracts may be put up for approval from different stakeholders, predominantly from the procurement and finance departments and occasionally from the user department
  • Some contractual negotiations with the supplier may also happen at this stage and thereafter, approved Contracts are released for usage for operational procurement
  • Business Roles  Purchaser/ Buyer, Sourcing Manager, Financial Controller

Output

Contract Management leads to approved Contracts being used as sources of supply for all subsequent purchases

Business Capabilities

Supplier Negotiation Management

  • Ability to prepare for the negotiation of contract terms with the supplier, to manage the negotiation process and record discussions and decisions, and to finalize negotiation and collect approvals

Operational Procurement Contract Management

  • Ability to manage the data from contracts necessary to process requisitions and create purchase orders, including supplier identification, products and services, price conditions, quantities, locations, etc
  • Includes contract compliance ability to comply with contracted prices and terms automatically across all operational procurement activities
  • For commodity related contracts, it includes flexible pricing rules based on different dimensions like market prices, qualities, quantities and locations

Purchase Price Management

  • Ability to manage purchasing cost, discounts and surcharges for products and services procured from a supplier
  • This includes the maintenance of purchase prices as well as the purchase price calculation in a specific context, for example in a purchase order or in an invoice
  • It also includes determination of commodity formulas linking to market prices

Purchasing Rebate Management

  • Ability to plan, monitor, and settle rebates in order to reduce purchase costs through the partial refunding of purchase prices
  • Includes the ability to determine the actual cost of purchasing rebate agreements with suppliers

Procurement Scheduling Agreement Management

  • Ability to create the schedule agreement and to initiate the delivery of a product from a supplier based on a schedule agreement, using dates and quantities as defined in the agreement

Supplier Catalog Management

  • Ability to allow supplier-initiated uploads of product and service catalogs
  • This typically involves catalog items that include product- and service-rich descriptions with category-specific characteristics, pictures, pricing conditions, and other related information

Procurement Contract Collaboration

  • Ability to collaborate, communicate, and work with suppliers throughout the contract lifecycle process

Commodity Risk Management

  • Ability to identify, quantify and manage commodity price risks
  • Includes dedicated commodity price risk reporting and the ability of managing hedging transactions
  • Covers accounting for the related financial transactions and positions according to international standards, including hedge accounting according to IFRS9

Legal Contract Lifecycle Management

  • Ability to manage the full lifecycle of contracts, from requesting contracts, to creating contracts, and storing, tracking, and searching for information from contracts

Distribution of Business Capabilities in the Contract Management Process

Solution Options

  1. SAP Ariba Sourcing
  2. SAP Ariba Procurement
  3. SAP Integration Suite, Managed Gateway for Spend Management
  4. SAP Business Network for Procurement (formerly Ariba Network)
  5. SAP S/4HANA (private and public cloud)
  6. SAP S/4 HANA for Supplier Quotation Management (S/4HANA SupQM)
  7. SAP Fieldglass Vendor Management System (Fieldglass VMS)
  8. SAP S/4 HANA for Central Procurement
  9. SAP Procurement Intelligence
  10. SAP Broker Reconciliation for Commodity Derivatives
  11. Icertis Contract Intelligence for SAP Ariba Solutions
  12. SAP Signature Management by DocuSign

Characterization of the Solution Options

Common Process Variants

Collaborative Contract Management with Suppliers

For more information, please refer to the best practice Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts (4AZ).

Event Based Collaborative Sourcing and Contract Management

For more information, please refer to the best practice Direct Material Sourcing (4RD), Preferred Supplier List (4RI) and Central Purchasing (2XT).

External Workforce and Service Procurement Contract Management

For more information, please refer to the topic “Workflow for SAP Ariba Contracts and SAP Fieldglass Integration” on help.sap.com.

Basic Contract Management

For more information, please refer to the best practice Purchase Contract (BMD).

Central Purchase Contracts without Collaboration

For more information, please refer to the best practice Central Purchase Contracts (2ME).

Central Purchase Contracts with Collaboration

For more information, please refer to the best practice Contract for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Contracts (4B0).

Capability Distribution Across E2E Process Variants

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

Solution Capability Based

Collaborative Contract Management with Suppliers

Event Based Collaborative Contract Management

Catalog and External Workforce

Commodities

Business Process/ IT Parameter Based

Additionally, the following business and IT considerations may also be applied in certain cases, to arrive at the most appropriate solution :

  1. Are there any regulatory/ strategic need to collaboratively manage negotiation with prospective suppliers for most purchases, e.g. for government/ public sectors?
  2. Is there a strong need to manage contract lifecycle within the system?
  3. Are collaborative negotiations limited largely to events like projects or marketing campaigns?
  4. Are there frequent occasions where the suppliers upload information about catalog items directly into the customer’s system?
  5. Is there large scale external workforce contract management?
  6. Is there significant procurement of commodity derivatives through brokers?
  7. Are contracts mostly required as an offline execution tool to manage supplier choice, quantity and value without the need for collaboration?
  8. Does the organization’s landscape consist of multiple ERPs from where specific items are chosen for centralized contracting?

Decision Tree

The architectural decision with the listed solution(s) are complementary and not mutually exclusive. Since the same set of business capabilities are sometimes enabled by multiple solution capabilities, the choice of application is tied more to the addressable business process variants than differential capabilities of the products.

Accordingly, the following 6 major E2E process variants (A – F) and 3 minor variants (G – I) discussed in the previous two sections may be used to arrive at application decisions :

  1. Collaborative Contract Management with Suppliers
  2. Event Based Collaborative Contract Management
  3. Basic Contract Management
  4. Central Purchase Contracts with Collaboration
  5. Central Purchase Contracts without Collaboration
  6. External Workforce Contract Management
  7. Supplier Catalog Collaboration
  8. Commodity Derivative Procurement
  9. Contract Lifecycle Management

 

Conclusion

  • For Basic Contract Management capabilities with no collaboration, SAP S/4HANA will suffice
  • For Event Based Sourcing and Contract Management, a combination of SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Supplier Quotation Management, SAP Procurement Intelligence and SAP Business Network for Procurement can work
  • For addressing full blown Collaborative Contract Management capabilities, a combination of SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba Sourcing, SAP Business Networks for Procurement and SAP Integration Suite Managed Gateway for Spend Management will be required
  • SAP S/4HANA Central Procurement will be required additionally, if Central Procurement capabilities are required, i.e. a central procurement hub procuring on behalf of connected back end ERP systems
  • For extensive External Workforce Procurement, SAP Fieldglass Vendor Management System will be required on top of the applications required to enable Collaborative Contract Management capabilities
  • For Catalog Collaboration with Suppliers, especially for indirect products and services, SAP Ariba Procurement will be needed
  • In addition to SAP S/4HANA Commodity Risk Management capabilities, for Commodity Derivative Procurement scenarios, SAP Broker Reconciliation for Commodity Derivatives should be used
  • If there is a need for managing contract lifecycle within the system, a combination of Ariba Sourcing, Icertis Contract Intelligence and Signature Management by DocuSign may be deployed

entry.title: Re: Only 0 qtly of material 2023 available ?
entry.content:
Hi,

Please see this SAP Note: 1513607

here are Cause
1. There is not enough quantity available for the confirmation of the delivery although you can see enough availablequantity in transaction MMBE or MD04.
2.  Different scope of checks (transaction OVZ9) for the preceding document (e.g. sales order) and follow-up document(delivery).
3. Negative cumulated ATP quantity in transaction CO09.
4. Sufficient available stock on storage location level but not on plant level.
5. ATP quantities are checked against a storage location in spite of no storage location has been determined in the predecessor sales document so the system should check available quantities on plant level only.
6. Inconsistencies.

Regards

entry.title: Re: CAP or RAP
entry.content:
Considering you skill set , you can choose either RAP or CAP Framework

As you are already familiar with ABAP , OData , maybe your learning curve for RAP will be faster than CAP.

( Note : These are my suggestions )

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP开发案例之五：采购申请批量上传
entry.content:
紧随这篇博客，我们将再次逐步详细地介绍一个利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud三系统来做开发扩展的案例。

 

1. 案例背景:

在这个案例中，我们会创建一个自定义的Fiori应用程序，通过该程序，我们可以从Excel文件批量创建多个采购申请记录。同时，该Excel导入模板可以根据我们的需求不同而完全自定义。

2. 后台服务开发

2.1 创建数据库表

为了保存从Excel文件上传的数据，我们首先创建一个数据库表：

定义一个表来保存Excel上传的数据

 

2.2 创建CDS数据模型

然后我们在数据库表之上创建CDS视图，作为与数据库交互的第一层。通过Annotations为所需字段配置不同的功能。

然后，我们在上一步创建的CDS视图之上再创建一个投影视图（projection view），作为一个独立的功能层，我们可以过滤特定的字段。

2.3 创建行为定义

 

然后创建行为实现类，当用户点击“Create Purchase Requisition”按钮时，将触发事件CREATE_PR，并通过EML创建采购申请。

 

2.4 创建服务定义和服务绑定

右键单击投影视图，接下来我们将创建一个新的服务定义。我们可以选择哪个实体将暴露于服务。由于我们只有一个实体，所以我们使用系统生成的代码。

 

然后右键单击服务定义来创建服务绑定：

 

在发布之后，我们可以预览该服务如下：

 

3. 前台应用开发

打开SAP Business Application Studio服务：

 

选择SAP Fiori Worklist Application：

选择我们之前创建的服务：

 

如下是该应用的文件结构：

对于Excel上传功能组件，可以参考以下博客：

Excel Upload using RAP: Part -1 | SAP Blogs

 

值得一提的是，我们在SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Store上有一个预交付的包，它也可以从本地Excel文件创建采购申请。详细链接如下：

Create Purchase Requisitions from Excel (48M)

然而，这两种方法的技术实现是不同的，在我们的例子中，我们使用的业务对象接口仅用于开发人员扩展性；对于iRPA，它使用公开发布的API。我们不能说一种方法比另一种更好，我们必须根据我们的情况选择合适的方法。

 

以上是该案例——采购申请批量创建自开发应用的步骤介绍。希望通过此案例的介绍能让您了解到三系统开发能实现的常见功能。

entry.title: SAP分析云2024年第一季度更新介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本博文介绍 SAP 分析云 2024 年第一季度的季度发布 (QRC) 的新功能和增强功能。

在下面的视频中，SAP 产品专家将向您展示 2024 年第一季度的部分重点功能。

 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本 1.0.395，以利用所有数据采集类型。

除以下情况外，您的代理需要 1.0.75 或以上版本：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库版本 1.0.99
  • SAP Universe (UNX)版本 1.0.91
  • SAP Business Warehouse (BW)版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 版本 1.0.235
  • File Server 版本 1.0.248

更多详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

每季度发布更新时，都需要通过 SNOTES 更新 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云的集成。这将确保 SAP BW 的后端和前端同步。要了解实施时需要哪些 SNOTES，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 中所附的 XML 文件。

有关将 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

为避免下载旧版本并可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的绑定不当带来的漏洞，只有三个最新版本可供下载。

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。

 

更新亮点

  • 系统和服务管理
    • 可信任域的外部超链接列表 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 二次 CMEK 轮换 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 切换到 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 中的高级计算引擎 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 仅发布变更通知 - 逐步停止计划将模型导出至文件库的功能副本
  • 增强分析
    • Just Ask：AI驱动的新一代自然语言查询（NLQ）|（SAP路线图浏览器）（博文）
    • 预测计划 - 了解单项预测的能力 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 假设模拟自动化 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 业务内容交付
    • 用于共识净收入规划、财务和销售规划以及流程洞察的业务内容包 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据中心可用性
    • 数据中心位于瑞士，可通过 Microsoft Azure 接入欧盟网络 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据发掘
    • 数据分析器
      • 创建计算度量（公式编辑器）|（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 在导出对话框中设置重复维成员打印选项 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW)：生成器面板中的维组支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 洞察的 URL API 支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据集成
    • 数据导出 API - 支持公共维 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 实时 SAP BW – 改进了对时间相关层次结构的支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 标准单位数据导出API
  • 数据建模
    • 通过上传数据创建新模型的能力 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 能够转换事实表中的数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 无主数据的维 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 根据另一个度量进行分解 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 模型计算中的 To-date 公式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计算工作区中对象之间关系的可视化表示 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 企业计划
    • 高级公式 – 支持计算成员 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 高级公式 - 版本维的变量成员支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 切换成员的 ID 或描述 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 以分析模型为源进行跨模型复制 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 现有分配的手动迁移选项 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划输入 - SAP BPC 实时数据源的流式数据输入支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 用于 SAP BTP 业务流程自动化的公用 API  |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 与Office 365同步日历事件 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 版本管理 - 在发布过程中显示无效的事实记录 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 撤消/重做以编辑 BPC 计划序列触发器微件
  • Microsoft Office 集成
    • Microsoft Excel 中的成员选择器的持续更新 - 选择范围 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 在 Microsoft Excel 中设置静态报告的格式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 支持 Microsoft Excel 中基于单元格的新自定义公式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • Microsoft Excel 设计器窗格标题的更新 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 故事设计
    • 应用带有内部（页面或故事）超链接的故事、页面、本地和链接分析筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 精简版查看器的首次发布 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 地图筛选器支持动态文本  |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 在优化故事体验中启用后台导出选项
    • 将优化故事导出至PowerPoint  |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 表 – 版本排序 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 实时 SAP BW 的基于动态时间的计算 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）（视频）
    • 变量支持 - 保存故事时不包括当前变量选择 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 将差异显示为瀑布图（适用于条形图/柱形图和水平瀑布图）|（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 故事计算
      • 交叉计算中的聚合
      • 从可用对象面板添加计算
    • 故事可扩展性
      • 自定义移动端的查看时工具栏 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 支持书签自定义微件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 使用文本池支持脚本翻译 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 获取和设置无值成员的输入控制 API |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 用户协作
      • 在应用了基于角色的数据安全的模型中进行留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 在新模型的受限交叉计算中启用数据点留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 移除发布故事时“无结束日期”选项 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
      • 在计划失败或部分成功时向用户发送通知 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 在表中使用维属性时，支持数据点留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • “包含格式”选项现在对计划发布 CSV 格式可用

 

系统和服务管理

可信任域的外部超链接列表

用户现在可以在被重定向到外部 URL 时启用警告信息。管理员可以创建免于警告的可信任域列表。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

 

切换到 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 中的高级计算引擎

  • 使用外壳栏导航菜单右上角的产品切换器菜单在 SAP分析云和 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 之间导航
  • 登录一次后无需重新验证即可再次访问服务

注：在 SAP 分析云的切换菜单中选择高级计算引擎时，外壳栏导航菜单会在新的浏览器选项卡中打开，显示 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 的入口页面。其 URL 定义在租户的系统管理页面的 "链接我的租户 "部分。 （SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

业务内容交付

用于共识净收入规划、财务和销售规划以及流程洞察的业务内容包

通过采用全新的预构建数据模型和故事，缩短实施 SAP 分析云所需的时间。

2024 年第一季度更新的行业和业务领域内容包包括：（SAP 路线图浏览器）

  • 新的行业和业务领域内容包：
    • 安全建议仪表板
    • SAP S/4HANA 投资组合财务规划
    • 共识净收入规划
    • 战略性劳动力规划
  • 更新的行业和业务领域内容包：
    • SAP S/4HANA 云计算的公共部门管理 KPI 报告
    • 公共部门管理规划
    • 商业规划
    • SAP S/4HANA 集成财务规划的跨模型功能
    • SAP S/4HANA 集团财务规划
    • SAP S/4HANA 集成财务规划
    • 扩展规划与分析 (XP&A) 和人力经验管理 (HXM)
    • 针对 SAP S/4HANA 和 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 的企业资产管理和工厂维护分析

点击此处了解有关 SAP Analytics 业务内容的更多信息。

 

数据中心可用性

数据中心位于瑞士，可通过 Microsoft Azure 接入欧盟网络

通过支持在瑞士的 Microsoft Azure 上部署，客户现在在数据中心位置和超级分销商平台方面有了更多选择。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

数据集成

数据导出 API - 支持公共维

  • 添加一个新的命名空间以显示公共维
  • 支持two-legged 和 three-legged OAuth 协议

注：适用于 Cloud Foundry 租户。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

企业计划

数据操作 - 以分析模型为源进行跨模型复制

使用跨模型复制操作时，能够将 SAP 分析云的分析（非规划）模型作为源模型读取数据。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

 

Microsoft Office 集成

Microsoft Excel 中的成员选择器的持续更新 - 选择范围

Excel 中扩展的成员选择器功能包括：

  • 选择一系列成员，将其定义为一个过滤器

只有在成员选择器中以 "平面列表模式 "显示成员时，该功能才可用。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

请注意，这只是预览版，将于 2024 年第一季度晚些时候发布。

 

在 Microsoft Excel 中设置静态报告的格式

  • 为表格提供新选项，允许用户停用默认表格格式并应用 Excel 样式
  • 停用默认表格格式时，用户可为插件表格添加 Excel 格式，并在执行 "刷新 "操作时保持格式不变
  • 用户可为静态表格创建高度自定义的格式；但这种格式不支持展开和折叠操作

（SAP 路线图浏览器）

该功能自 2024 年 1 月 31 日起可用。

 

Microsoft Excel 设计器窗格标题的更新

  • 通过设计器窗格页眉显示工作表中所选对象的详细内容，使用户能够更改对象，如表格、数据操作触发器或用于公式的数据源。
  • 对标题进行额外更新，以丰富信息和导航功能：
    • 用工作表中显示的对象（表格、数据操作触发器或公式的数据源别名）的详细信息丰富标题中的信息

（SAP 路线图浏览器）

该功能自 2024 年 1 月 31 日起可用。

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和增强功能的高级概述。有关这些功能的更多详细信息，请访问 SAP 分析云帮助门户。您还可以访问 SAP 路线图浏览器查看 SAP 分析云即将推出的功能。

entry.title: OUT NOW: SAP Signavio February 2024 release
entry.content:
We are excited to announce today the general availability of our SAP Signavio February 2024 release. 

Whether you’re transforming your organization or scaling up to the next level of operational excellence, the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite February release provides you with important benefits. The focus of this release is introducing the ability to connect strategic objectives to execution, build cohesive variant management, and carry out lasting results with trendline monitoring  

With the release you are able to: 

  • Achieve more outcomes by connecting strategic objectives to business execution  
  • Simplify and elevate their process landscape with Variant Management that brings structure and transparency  
  • And can track and analyze trendlines to identify real progress or shortcomings 

You will find a summary of the main highlights below. 

SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Signavio Process Governance

Elevate your process management to new heights with this groundbreaking release. Simplify orchestration, foster collaboration with enhanced transparency, and take full control of your process model repositories. Stay ahead of the curve in the competitive market landscape and experience efficiency and precision like never before. 


Variant Management- SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Signavio Process Governance

We are excited to announce a transformative enhancement to SAP Signavio which solidifies its position as a frontrunner in Business Process Transformation. Variant Management is a capability designed to ease your process transformation efforts and enhance management of complex process landscapes, enabled by SAP Signavio Process Manager, which is responsible for the bulk of content creation and preview features this capability offers, and supported by SAP Signavio Process Governance, which ensures changes are propagated 

It allows process managers, owners, and modelers to effectively manage the multitude of variants in processes by identifying and modeling variants, controlling their relationships, reducing redundancy, tracking and propagating changes, and supporting users in consuming the right variant depending on the context and the role. 

click here for a demo :
Variant Management: Create templates, attach variants, manage dimensions & values
Variant Management: Clone process to create a variant and detach variants from templates
Variant Management: Propagation of automatic changes

Variant Management: Propagation of manual changes

 

SAP Signavio Process Insights 

 With the February 2024 release, SAP Signavio Process Insights is releasing the following:

  • Trendlines to visualize process performance 
  • Integration with SAP Supplier Relationship Management and SAP Extended Warehouse Management as a source system 
  • Save filter selection 
  • Direct link to source system 
  • Connecting multiple SAP ECC systems to one intermediate system 
  • Translation to Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Korean, German, French, Portuguese, and Spanish  

Trendlines to visualize process performance  


We are pleased to announce the release of trendlines which show users a visual representation of performance over time. Trendlines are designed to accelerate time to analysis and help a wide range of stakeholders to gain a better understanding of process performance. This new update helps you as process owner and transformation driver to identify peaks, anomalies, and patterns in operations at a glance. In addition it supports you as a process owner to share contextualized updates and process performance. 

Process performance trendlines – SAP Signavio Process Insights

Click here for a demo: Trendlines to visualize process performance

Integration with SAP Supplier Relationship Management as a source system  


The first of 2 integrations we are announcing in this release is integration with SAP Supplier Relationship Management as a source system. SAP Supplier Relationship Management (SAP SRM) provides you with innovative methods to coordinate your business processes with your key suppliers and make them more effective. Additionally, SAP SRM enables you to optimize your procurement strategy, to work more effectively with your supplier pool, and gain long-term benefits from your supplier relationships.  

With the new connection, process owners and transformation drivers can analyze and improve their performance of source-to-pay processes running in SAP SRM. This will allow you yo get fact-based insights on usage in your SAP SRM, for sourcing as well as operation procurement. 

 

Integration with SAP Extended Warehouse Management as a source system

 
SAP Signavio Process Insights now connects to SAP Extended Warehouse Management. The new integration to SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM) allows process owners and transformation drivers to analyse and improve performance running in SAP EWM. This allows you to obtain fact-based insights into what is usage in SAP EWM, for inbound and outbound processes. You are also able to gain support for your transformation initatives such as a move to SAP S/4HANA. 
 

Save filter selection 


Based on roles and evolving objectives, as process owner and transformation driver you need different filtered views of process flows and metrics that you can access quickly and intuitively. You need to come back to certain analytical steps to review the status or further drive analysis. With the February release, you will now be able to save selected filters and filter values, which they will then be able to share with colleagues in a few short clicks. This update will enable you to keep track of insights and return to them more easily, while also reducing repetitive tasks and highlighting important pages. This also makes access of previously investigated issues easierand quicker. 

Save filter selection – SAP Signavio Process Insights

Click here for a demo: Ability to save filter selection

Direct link to source system  


The direct link to source systems such as SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition now offers you a streamlined document access and efficient issue resolution. By enabling direct navigation from a list of documents in Signavio Process Insights to specific documents in these source systems, users can swiftly access additional information where needed. This integration not only enhances efficiency but also facilitates quicker issue resolution, as users can easily drill down to individual documents, gaining detailed insights and expediting problem-solving processes. Embracing this integration empowers you with a faster, more convenient approach to document access and troubleshooting, ultimately driving productivity and effectiveness across the organization. 

Link directly to ERP source systems – SAP Signavio Process Insights

Connecting multiple SAP ECC systems to one intermediate system  


In the August 2023 release we announced that you can connect a single instance of your SAP ECC system (on any enhancement package) to SAP Signavio Process Insights via an intermediate system in a one-to-one relationship. We are pleased to follow up this previous release by introducing the ability to connect not one, but multiple SAP ECC systems, on any enhancement package, to SAP Signavio Process Insights - many SAP ECC systems to one intermediate system.  

This update decreases your time to connect to an intermediate system, while also reducing the high manual effort, maintenance, and support required for multiple intermediate systems. 

 

Translation to Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Korean, German, French, Portuguese, and Spanish  


The February release sees the introduction of eagerly anticipated translations in Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Korean, German, French, Portuguese and Spanish. The introduction of these languages is aimed at improving accessibility and ease of use for non-English speaking users by providing a localized version of SAP Signavio Process Insights. The different languages are available from the menu, under Language. 

Language options – SAP Signavio Process Insights

Click here for a demo: Switch to different languages

SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition 

 

Interested in analyzing your processes but haven't begun? SAP Signavio Process Insights, disovery edition offers a starting point to examine your initial process, assess its performance, and benchmark it. The Discovery edition aids in identifying obstacles, suggests quick solutions, and showcases SAP's innovations that could elevate your operations. This could also serve as a stepping stone towards transitioning to S/4HANA. Try SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition at no cost by visiting url.sap/discovery-edition or by contacting your SAP Signavio representative. 

 

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub 

 
E-mail notifications related to insights from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and commenting use case 


Stay in the loop with e-mail notifications regarding comments and your insights from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. Users receive an e-mail notification anytime insights are assigned, modified, or deleted. They are also notified whenever they are mentioned in a comment, they receive a reply, or someone comments on their diagrams or models. Users have the power to control these notifications according to their preferences directly in the personal settings in SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub. 

E-mail notifications related to insights from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

click here for a demo: E-mail notifications related to insights from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and commenting use case

New user interface 


As part of the incremental updates to the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite User Interface, we are happy to announce the redesign of SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub UI. The new design is aimed at improving the usability of SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub, as well as provide a seamless user experience across the whole suite.

New user interface – SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 

With the February 2024 relase for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence we are releasing the following: 

  • New actions design 
  • New user interface to configure data extraction 
  • Value accelerator for managing transportation execution in SAP Transportation Management 

 
New Actions Design  


With the introduction of a new Actions Design, we are aiming to simplify user navigation and enhance usability. With the redesigned actions feature, users no longer need to navigate outside the Process to view their list of Actions. The implementation has been streamlined and simplified, making it more accessible for a broader range of users with a user-friendly and intuitive interface. 

New actions design - SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
 New user interface to configure data extraction  


With our new update, configuring the data extraction logic has never been easier. The redesigned "Source Data" configuration page, complete with a convenient side panel for quick access to the table list, simplifies the process. What's more, users can now select multiple tables for extraction simultaneously, saving valuable time. These enhancements significantly reduce the complexity and time required to configure data extraction, ensuring a smooth and efficient user experience. 

Interface for data extraction – SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Click here for demo: New user interface to configure data extraction

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler 
 

Journey Model Templates  


Effortless journey modeling can be a challenging task for journey modelers. This new feature enables you as SAP Signavio Journey Modeler admin to create central journey templates or save existing journeys as templates, giving journey modelers the ability to choose from a wide range of templates as a starting point instead of starting from zero, and design the journey following the template with mandatory and optional sections. With this feature, you can benefit from a standardized journey library, reduced time and effort to design a journey, and re-use best practice journeys. 

 

Journey model templates – SAP Signavio Journey Modeler

SAP Signavio Process Governance 

Comments for Process Rating 


With the new feature to comment process ratings you as Process owner and modeler can already receive quantitative feedback on diagrams within the current process rating feature. However process modelers also need additional context from the feedback provided and more details on the reasonings behind the rating. 

Comments for Process Rating – SAP Signavio Process Governance

SAP Signavio Value Accelerators 

SAP Signavio Process Insights - New value accelerator for analysis of SAP S/4HANA Service 


SAP S/4HANA Service supports you with the setup and operation of functions and processes for service contract management, business solution portfolio management, service order management, service request management, and more 

This value accelerator consists of:  

  • metrics and process flows that increase transparency and help improve professional service processes, e.g., of service contract management 

Value accelerator for analysis of SAP S/4HANA Service

Click here for a demo: Value Accelerator for analysis of SAP S/4HANA Service

SAP Signavio Process Insights - New value accelerator for analysis of SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Procurement 


Spend management solutions from the SAP Ariba portfolio empower you to move faster and spend better. As a buyeryou can better manage the purchasing process to achieve efficiency and savings. 

This value accelerator consists of: 

  • Process flows and process performance indicators for SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Procurement 
  • Customers gain better understanding of process performance and ultimately improve procurement and spending efficiency 

Value accelerator for analysis of SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Procurement

Click here for a demo: Value Accelerator for analysis of SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Signavio Process Insights - New value accelerator for Automation Rates for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere 


The challenge for many organizations is making the right insights and metrics available to the right stakeholders. This value accelerator makes it easy and fast for you to publish automation rates to SAP Analytics Cloud/SAP Datasphere 

This value accelerator delivers: 

  • Easy means of connecting SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Analytics Cloud/SAP Datasphere 
  • Predefined connectors and stories for automation rates in the SAP Analytics Cloud content network 
  • Customers can improve collaboration on automation rates with shared insights, 
  • reduce analysis time and resolve issues faster 

Value accelerator for Automation Rates for SAP Analytics Cloud & SAP Datasphere

 

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence - New value accelerator for managing transportation execution in SAP Transportation Management 


This powerful accelerator provides stakeholders with faster time to value by analyzing processes, tackling the use cases of cancelled freight orders, carrier behavior, and automation. With a templated approach to get started quickly, users can then make customizations according to their individual needs.  

By leveraging this value accelerator, organizations gain visibility into transportation processes, identify automation opportunities, streamline workflows, and reduce manual efforts. This not only mitigates cancelled orders but also ensures efficient resource utilization. Furthermore, insights derived from process mining empower organizations to assess and improve carrier performance, fostering a more agile and responsive transportation ecosystem.  

This value accelerator for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence delivers: 

  • Prebuilt data transformation templates, metrics, and dashboard templates 
  • Customers can jump start the mining experience and accelerates the benefits of process mining:  
  • Increase visibility into the transportation processes 
  • Identify automation opportunities 
  • Streamline workflows and reduce manual efforts 
  • Better resource utilization, e.g. increased carrier performance 

Value accelerator for managing transportation execution in SAP Transportation Management

Click here for a demo: Value Accelerator for managing transportation execution in SAP Transportation Management

Plug and gain approach value accelerators 

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite - New value accelerator for analysis of Finance processes in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach) 


A company’s liquidity, efficiency, and profitability can be improved by achieving the objective of “optimize capital effectiveness.”  

In this context, best practices and process performance content can help you to redesign and optimize the relevant processes, such as invoice to pay or invoice to cash.  

This value accelerator includes industry-agnostic content focusing on optimizing working capital for SAP S/4HANA. 

It delivers: 

  • Strategic objectives and metrics mapped to best practices in the SAP Signavio Process Manager 
  • Process models and value chains 
  • Navigation maps and visuals (value chain diagrams) 
  • Dictionary entries 

Starting from a clear business objective (working capital optimization) you  can: 

  • Get inspiration on how to redesign the processes, associate metrics to measure, and the recommended IT applications and initiatives  
  • Build on best practices to mitigate risks and achieve business goals 

Value accelerator: for analysis of Finance processes in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach)

Click here for a demo: Value Accelerator for analysis of Finance processes in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach)

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite - New value accelerator: for analysis of acquire to decommission in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach) 


Similarly to previous, in the context of plug and gain, this new value accelerator  supports the analysis of the acquire to decommission process. By leveraging this value accelerator, you can better understand your acquire to decommission process performance.  

This value accelerator for the analysis of the acquire to decommission process delivers:  

  • process flows, process flow variants, metrics and dashboards for the analysis of acquire to decommission 

Value accelerator: for analysis of acquire to decommission in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach

Click here for a demo: Value Accelerator for analysis of Acquire to Decommission in SAP ERP (plug and gain approach)

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Update: Value accelerator for  SAP ERP process  lanscape analysis (plug and gain approach) 

With this release, we support the analysis of asset accounting and retail processes in SAP ERP leveraging the plug and gain approach.  

The process house has been updated to include asset accounting and retail-specific process-execution models, execution analysis, variant analysis, and transaction analysis, covering asset master creation to deactivation. 

You can gain greater transparency into asset accounting and retail processes, enabling the acceleration of transformation initiatives supported in SAP ERP 

 Update: Value Accelerator for SAP ERP process landscape analysis (plug and gain approach)

Click here for a demo: Update: Value Accelerator for SAP ERP process landscape analysis (plug and gain approach)
 
Partnerships and Integrations 

Knoa SolEx partnership 

SAP UEM by Knoa enables you to analyze business processes at a deeper level. Granular user activity logs enrich process data with detailed task and user behavior analytics 

You can find all details on the knoa partnership here 

Summary 

Whether you're undergoing a complete overhaul or gearing up for expansion, the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite February release offers invaluable benefits. 

Here's a quick overview of what this release has in store: 

  • Align strategic objectives with execution: Seamlessly connect your strategic objectives with day-to-day business execution, ensuring that every action contributes to your overarching goals. 
  • Enhance process landscape with vVariant management: Simplify and elevate your process landscape with Variant Management, bringing much-needed structure and transparency to complex workflows. 
  • Drive lasting results with trendline monitoring: Track and analyze trendlines to gain insights into your progress and identify areas for improvement, ensuring that your transformation efforts yield tangible results. 

 
With these new features, you can achieve more than ever before. Empower your organization to thrive in today's rapidly changing business landscape.  

This blog post only provides an overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite with our February 2024 release. For more details, please read our release notes.  

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello @DCharanRaj warm welcome to SAP Community. Great that you are now part of it. As you are from India, I would like to point you to the already existing location groups around India. You can find them here. When you found one, which is close to where you live, you can simply click the blue "join" button and you are part of it! Once there are local activities, you find out via the group, or you can even connect with other group members on your own. Furthermore, the SAP Learning Groups are a great starting point here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hallo @Abbym willkommen in der SAP Community!

We use to communicate in English here, to give everyone the opportunity to follow the conversation. That is also the reason I am answering in English. 😉

As you mentioned, you are not sure how to start with SAP, let me please point you to these main components of SAP Community:

  • Topic pages: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics to get to know about SAP products
  • Blog posts: https://community.sap.com/t5/all-sap-community-blogs/ct-p/all-blogs to read experience and knowledge from other community members
  • Discussion Groups: https://community.sap.com/t5/interest-groups/ct-p/interests to get in touch to each other and discuss specific topics
  • What's New: https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/bg-p/whats-new to find all about recent or upcoming news

Simply choose the three-dot-menu and click "subscribe". Set your notification settings accordingly, so that you get notified about new content published!

entry.title: create a new product catalog sync that perform only synchronisation of pricerow
entry.content:
Hi,

one requirement of my project is the only synchronisation of priceRow because them are imported from an external system, while the products (and other items) must follow the canonical way.

I tried to create a new syncJob with the impex below;

I understood that for select items and attributes that are needed to be sync I must work on attributeDescriptor but I don't know how.

 

$solPrefix = myProject
    
INSERT_UPDATE CatalogVersionSyncJob ; code[unique=true,path-delimiter=!]      ;sourceVersion(catalog(id),version)     ;targetVersion(catalog(id),version)     ; syncPrincipals(uid) ; removeOnExit ; sessionUser(uid) ; syncLanguages(isocode) 
                                    ; sync $solPrefixPriceRow:Staged->Online  ;$solPrefixProductCatalog:Staged        ;$solPrefixProductCatalog:Online        ; syncuser            ; true         ; syncuser         ; en,it
 
 
INSERT_UPDATE SyncAttributeDescriptorConfig ; syncJob(code)[unique=true,path-delimiter=!]  ; attributeDescriptor(enclosingType(code),qualifier)[unique=true] ; includedInSync ; copyByValue
                                            ; sync $solPrefixPriceRow:Staged->Online 	   ; priceRow:products                                           ; false          ; false    		 
#                                            ; sync $solPrefixPriceRow:Staged->Online 	   ; Category:products                                           ; false          ; false    		 

 

the run of the impex return the error:

									
INSERT_UPDATE SyncAttributeDescriptorConfig;syncJob(code)[unique=true,path-delimiter=!];attributeDescriptor(enclosingType(code),qualifier)[unique=true];includedInSync;copyByValue
,,,,error finding existing item : column='attributeDescriptor' value='priceRow:products', | column 2: could not resolve item for priceRow:products;sync myprojectPriceRow:Staged->Online;priceRow:products;false;false

That I need to put on attributeDescriptor for select only priceRow?

And (in general) how works the multivalue referenced types?

Anyone have tips?

Marco

entry.title: Re: Inventory Posting List Default Posting Dates Range
entry.content:
Hi,

because of the problems in the past, I made this query.
Perhabs it fits to your needs

/*Ersetzt die Bestandsbuchungsliste*/
/*Datum von/bis ist optional*/
/*Lager ist optional*/
/*Author LoH*/
/*Update: 20240111*/

IF OBJECT_ID ('tempdb..#MoveData','U') IS NOT NULL
DROP TABLE dbo.#MoveData

IF OBJECT_ID ('tempdb..#SumData','U') IS NOT NULL
DROP TABLE dbo.#SumData

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T0 **/
DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateFrom = /* T0.F_RefDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OFPR] T1 **/
DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @DocDateTo = /* T1.T_RefDate */ '[%1]'

/**SELECT FROM [OITM] T2 **/
DECLARE @ItemCode AS NVARCHAR(max)
/* WHERE */
SET @ItemCode = /* T2.ItemCode */ '[%2]'

/**SELECT FROM [OIVL] T3 **/
DECLARE @WhsCode AS NVARCHAR(max)
/* WHERE */
SET @WhsCode = /* T3.LocCode */ '[%3]'

--/*DEBUG ONLY*/
--DECLARE @DocDateFrom AS Date
--DECLARE @DocDateTo AS Date
--DECLARE @ItemCode AS NVARCHAR(max)
--DECLARE @WhsCode AS NVARCHAR(max)
--SET @DocDateFrom = '20220330'
--SET @DocDateTo = '20240116'
--SET @ItemCode ='BZ1024'
--SET @WhsCode = ''

/*Für den Where-Filter unten wird ein Datum benötigt*/
IF @DocDateTo = ''
	BEGIN
		SELECT @DocDateTo = '20991231'
	END

/*Hole alle Bewegungsdaten für den Artikel*/
SELECT
	ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY '' ORDER BY [OIVL].[Itemcode],[OIVL].[DocDate]) AS [LineNum]
	,[OIVL].[ItemCode]
	,CASE
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 15		THEN 'Lieferung'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 16		THEN 'Retoure VK' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 13		THEN 'Ausgangsrechnung' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 14		THEN 'Ausgangsgutschrift'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 132		THEN 'Ausgangskorrekturrechnung'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 20		THEN 'Wareneingang EK'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 21		THEN 'Retoure VK'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 18		THEN 'Eingangsrechnung' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 19		THEN 'Eingangsgutschrift'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = -2		THEN 'Eröffnungsbilanz'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 58		THEN 'Lagerjournal'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 59		THEN 'Man. Wareneingang' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 60		THEN 'Man. Warenausgang'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 67		THEN 'Bestandsumlagerung' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 68		THEN 'Produktionsanweisungen' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = -1		THEN 'Alle Transaktionen'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 162		THEN 'Bestandsneubewertung' 
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 69		THEN 'Wareneinstandspreise'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 310000001	THEN 'Anfangsbestand'
	WHEN [OIVL].[TransType] = 10000071	THEN 'Bestandsbuchung' 
	ELSE CAST([OIVL].TransType AS nvarchar(max)) + ' ist unbekannt' 
	END AS [DocType]
	,[OIVL].[DocDate]
	,[OIVL].[TransType]
	,[OIVL].[BASE_REF] AS DocEntry
	,[OIVL].[DocLineNum]
	,[OIVL].[LocCode] AS WhsCode
	,[OIVL].[InQty]
	,[OIVL].[OutQty] 

INTO [dbo]..[#MoveData]
FROM 
	[OIVL]

WHERE 
	([OIVL].DocDate <= @DocDateTo OR @DocDateTo = '')
	AND 
	([OIVL].ItemCode = @ItemCode /*OR @ItemCode = ''*/)
	AND 
	(OIVL.LocCode = @WhsCode OR @WhsCode = '')
	AND
	([OIVL].InQty > 0 OR [OIVL].OutQty > 0)

/*Wenn das Startdatum nicht leer ist muss eine Summe berechet werden damit die Endsumme passt*/
IF @DocDateFrom <> ''
	BEGIN
		SELECT
		DISTINCT
		 0 AS [LineNum] 
		,[MD].[ItemCode]
		,'Summe Datum kleiner ->' AS [DocType]
		,@DocDateFrom AS [DocDate]
		,0 AS [TransType]
		,'' AS [DocEntry]
		,0 AS [DocLineNum]
		,'' AS [WhsCode]
		,ISNULL(SUM([MD].[InQty]),0) AS [InQty]
		,ISNULL(SUM([MD].[OutQty]),0) AS [OutQty]
		,ISNULL(SUM(ISNULL([MD].[InQty],0) - ISNULL([MD].[OutQty],0)),0) AS [SumTotal]
		INTO [dbo]..[#SumData]
		FROM
			[dbo]..[#MoveData] MD
		WHERE
			[MD].[DocDate] < @DocDateFrom
		GROUP BY 
			[MD].[ItemCode]
	END

/*Wenn ein Startdatum gesetzt ist, hole die Summe als Anzeige und dann den Rest der Daten*/
IF @DocDateFrom <> ''

	BEGIN
		/*Summe*/
		SELECT 
			*
		FROM 
			[dbo]..[#SumData] SD
	
	UNION ALL
	
	SELECT 
		/*Restdaten*/
		[MoDa].*
		,SUM([MoDa].[InQty] - [MoDa].[OutQty]) OVER (ORDER BY [MoDa].[LineNum])  + [SD].[SumTotal] /*Die Summe wird hinzugerechnet damit es passt*/
	FROM 
		[dbo]..[#MoveData] [MoDa]
		INNER JOIN [dbo]..[#SumData] [SD] ON [SD].[ItemCode] = [MoDa].[ItemCode]
	WHERE 
		[MoDa].[DocDate] >= @DocDateFrom 
		AND 
		[MoDa].[DocDate] <= @DocDateTo
	ORDER BY 
		[LineNum] ASC
	END

ELSE
/*Ansonsten nimm alle Daten*/
	BEGIN
		SELECT 
			*
			,SUM([MoDa].[InQty] - [MoDa].[OutQty]) OVER (ORDER BY [MoDa].[LineNum]) AS [SumTotal]
		FROM 
			[dbo]..[#MoveData] [MoDa]
		ORDER BY 
			[MoDa].[LineNum] ASC
	END

regards Lothar

entry.title: Repetitive manufacturing Profile
entry.content:
Repetitive manufacturing (REM) profile is one of mandatory master data for Repetitive manufacturing process. It controls how-to carry-on planning and confirmation in Repetitive manufacturing process.

 

However, REM profile could create through system configuration as per business process.

 

Configuration:

 

REM Profile has below mentioned sections.

  1. Control Data1
  2. Control Data2
  3. Movement types

 

Let’s discuss on Control data1.

 

GI backflush at GR posting – This check box control whether BOM components consume at the same time when goods receipt happens.

 

RP confirmations -This would use in case of Make to stock manufacturing and long lead time. Consumption of components could be recorded at a time that is close to time of operations to which it assigned. This help to calculate value of WIP products.

 

Reporting point could also use to manufacture with /without Kanban.

 

This is two types Mandatory Reporting point and Optional reporting Point.

 

Mandatory reporting point: This should use if quantity of production line, need to confirm along with each report point.

 

Optional reporting -This should use if quantity of production needs to confirm at the end of Production process.

 

 

Automatic GR Posting at Last RP when backflushing: This will control to do goods receipt during backflush of Last reporting point.

 

Process Control: This help separate below process from goods receipt. This could config under node of separate backflushing process.

                                            1.Goods issue

                                             2.Activity posting.

 

Error correction for Backflushing: This will generate backlog of errors during backflush of components.

 

 

Execute correction in dialogue mode: With this, system would ask to fix backflush error immediately during confirmation instead of generating entries in COGI.

With Mandatory option, this would not allow to goods receipt without fixing backflush errors.

With Optional option, it would generate pop-up with all backflush error. But that could skip and post confirmation. Then it would generate entries in COGI.

 

 

Let’s discuss on Control data2.

 

Planned order Reduction: Reduce planned order of material with production version and that of material without production version.

 

Reduction Period: This is calculated by adding the number of days you enter here to the backflush date. The system reduces all planned orders and run schedule quantities in the period of reduction.

 

Firming Logic:

With option “Always firm”- Update firming indicator in planned order which generate through Planning table.

 

With option “Do not firm”- Do not update firming indicator in planned order which generate through Planning table.

 

With option “Firm within plng time fence”-Update firming indicator in planned order if it falls with planning time fence of material.

 

Create planned orders when reversing: Will re-create planned order which were reduced during backflush, when reversing backflush.

 

 

Aggregate requirements: This will decide whether all dependent requirement of component grouped together to total requirement for a day when there is various requirement for that component on that day.

 

 

Movement type section has all movement types such as 261,262,131,132 etc.

entry.title: Empowering Process Transformation: Variant Management for Managing Complex Process Landscapes
entry.content:
It is with great excitement that we announce a transformative enhancement to SAP Signavio, solidifying its position as a frontrunner in Business Process Transformation. Variant Management is a capability designed to ease your process transformation efforts and enhance the management of complex process landscapes. 

The Power of Variant Management 

In the ever-evolving and complex process landscapes, having transparency in process repositories is critical, and managing the multitude of process variants is a key to better process management. SAP Signavio's Variant Management is a game-changer, offering an array of positive impacts on your process transformation journey brought to you via SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Signavio Process Governance. 

Understanding Variant Management 

A process variant is a distinct version of how a specific process is executed in diverse setups. These variations may emerge due to factors like geographical locations, customer channels, business units, or any unique characteristic that prompts the need for process customization. These variants differ in elements such as process sequence, roles and responsibilities, IT systems, documents, and other process attributes.

Variant Management is managing the multitude of variants in processes by both identification and modeling of variants, controlling their relationships & reducing redundancy, tracking & propagating changes from a process to its variants, and supporting users with consuming the right variant.   

With the February 2024 release, SAP Signavio provides Variant Management to make process transformation even easier. The added value encompasses the following capabilities: 

  • Identification & modeling of variants: clearly defining and representing each process variant in the process repository. 
  • Controlling relationships & reducing redundancy: managing how variants relate to each other and minimizing duplication to enhance efficiency. 
  • Tracking & propagating changes: enabling the seamless tracking of changes within a process and propagating these changes to associated variants. 

How does Variant Management support process transformation? 

The ability to manage these variants efficiently translates to: 

  1. Adaptable processes for a diverse landscape: Users can now create, identify, and manage process variants to meet the unique needs and requirements arising from different geographical locations, customer channels, and business units. This adaptability ensures that processes remain finely tuned to specific contextual requirements, fostering a responsive approach to changes. 
  2. Compliance management without compromise: Variant Management assists in adhering to diverse compliance requirements and to local regulations. 
  3. Seamless scalability without disruption: Track and propagate changes across processes and variants, maintaining control over global and local operations without disrupting existing workflows, ensuring a harmonized approach to operations. 
  4. Transparent and multitude management of variants: Manage multiple variants for a particular process within a transparent repository by connecting and streamlining interconnected process variants, reducing redundancy, and ensuring consistency across your documentation. 

Unlocking possibilities: Variant Management use cases 

The versatility of Variant Management extends across various business scenarios, delivering significant value to industries and organizations facing unique challenges. These are a few example applications of variant management that you may consider: 

  1. Managing geographical and regional variants: different countries and regions bring distinct compliance requirements, demanding a tailored approach. For multinational companies, navigating processes across diverse global locations is simplified with Variant Management. It ensures adherence to local regulations while minimizing unnecessary variations, providing a standardized framework that eases compliance complexities. 
  2. Harmonizing products and brands: organizations with diverse product portfolios (e.g., manufacturing companies) face the challenge of managing processes for different products and brands. Variant Management in SAP Signavio allows organizations to streamline these processes, ensuring harmonization across various sites with unique capabilities and procedures. 
  3. Managing Variants at different organizational levels: within a single region, different units often operate with specific process variants. In large corporate structures, like holdings, central best practices are defined. Variant Management facilitates a seamless adaptation of these templates, allowing different units to enroll and adapt the processes to their specific regional needs (e.g., in HR processes). 
  4. Managing Variants of different served industries: corporate entities catering to diverse industries encounter varied process requirements. Variant Management becomes instrumental in efficiently managing these process variants, ensuring that each variant aligns with the unique demands and standards of the industries served, fostering adaptability and efficiency. 
  5. Managing Customer Type Variants: businesses dealing with diverse customer types require the introduction of variants based on different channels used by customers. Variant Management facilitates the efficient handling of these customer type variants, ensuring processes are tailored to meet the unique needs and preferences of each customer segment.  

Embrace the Future with SAP Signavio 

With Variant Management, SAP Signavio is not just making process transformation easier; it is opening doors to innovation, efficiency, and adaptability. In essence, Variant Management in SAP Signavio is not just a feature—it’s a strategic capability designed to simplify and elevate your process transformation experience with a range of features across the SAP Signavio Transformation Suite.  

Here is what Variant Management encompasses - high-level: 

In the Variant Management Center, Global and Local Process Owners collaborate seamlessly to manage Templates and Variants, forming the backbone of Variant Groups. "Variant Groups" are established by the Global Process Owner, who sets the global version as a template, attaching diagrams as variants and defining applicable dimensions and values. Both Global and Local Process Owners can attach or detach variants, offering flexibility and control. 

3-1 Creating Template & Variants and forming the process landscape structure 

Variants can be cloned from templates or other variants, inheriting default dimensions and values. Cloning from a regular diagram transforms it into a template, creating a new Variant Group. This feature streamlines the process and ensures consistency across diagrams.

Figure1. identify a process as a template

3-2 Managing Dimensions and Values 

In the conceptual phase, Transformation Managers list possible dimensions and allocate values to variants. The Variant Management Center enables bulk management of dimensions and values for the entire Variant Group. Global and privileged Local Process Owners control and modify dimension values, fostering consistency and adaptability. 

Figure2. set dimensions & values to process templates and variants

Figure3. manage dimensions & values of selected models in the Variant Management Center

3-3 Managing Change Among Variants via Propagation 

Propagating changes across variants ensures a harmonized approach to process modifications. The Variant Management center facilitates global and local process owners in tracking and propagating alterations, maintaining control over global and local operations without disrupting workflows. This feature ensures a synchronized and adaptable operational landscape, contributing to efficient change management across diverse process variants.  

Figure4. monitor changes, evaluate, and decide on accepting/rejecting changes

Do you want to learn more about Variant Management? here are some useful links:

  • Variant Management Overview
  • SAP help: Variant Management
  • Introduction to Variant Management by SAP Signavio

entry.title: [Error] Ledger Z2 defined in CoCd JB01 is not active in chart of depreciation 1110
entry.content:
When I tried to create a PO based on an Asset, I got the following error:

Ledger Z2 defined in CoCd JB01 is not active in chart of depreciation 1110

Message no. ACC_AA096

Diagnosis

Ledger Z2 is assigned to company code JB01.

However, this ledger is not used in any ledger group that is assigned to a depreciation area of chart of depreciation 1110.

System Response

  • The system issues this error message if you execute program RASFIN_MIGR_PRECHECK.
  • If you make changes in Customizing, they cannot be saved.
  • If you post a document, the posting is terminated.

Procedure

Check the ledger assigned to your company code; check it in the general ledger and in Asset Accounting (in the chart of depreciation).

 

Appreciate your support on this.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Graph storing the data
entry.content:
Hi Keerthana,

Sorry for the delay answering your question. Please find the answers bellow:

1. So your last assumption is correct. Graph doesn't store data from underlying business systems. We fetch data from the data sources and provide the response.

2. Based on the first answer, on each call to Graph you will get the latest/live data from underlying business system. During the creation of BDG we fetch metadata from underlying systems. If there is a change in the metadata(new entity defined) you need to reprocess the BDG in order to have the latest model. This can be done easily through the BDG details UI.

3. Graph adds additional projections on top of the mirrored entities from supported SAP systems, which we call unified entities. Unlike the mirrored entities, these are thoughtfully designed and constructed projections, created by SAP experts under the reserved namespace sap.graph. Unified entities follow the SAP One Domain Model compatibility guidelines and are designed to bridge and connect semantically common business concepts from multiple data sources (for example, Business Partner, and Product). This allows client apps to get started with cross-system queries.

Please feel free to reach out if you have more questions and need help. We would be happy to help you.

Best regards,

Hamid, Product Owner - Graph 

Links:

Graph Documentation

Graph example in Business Accelerator Hub

entry.title: Re: Groovy script udf to handling script length
entry.content:
hi learninghubx01 ,

Below code will work for you

import com.sap.it.api.mapping.*;

def void StringLen(String[] arg1, Output output, MappingContext context) {
    if (arg1 != null && arg1.length > 0) {
        def str = arg1[0]
        for (int i = 0; i < arg1.length; i++) { 
            if (arg1[i].length() >= 13) {
                output.addValue(arg1[i].substring(0, 13))
            } else {
                output.addValue(arg1[i])
            }
        }
    }
}

Regards,

Satheesh Kumar.

entry.content:

diCompany.LicenseServer = Glo.LicenseServer;

Still receiving the "-147 Get CurrentUserInfo failed" error on SAP10 connection. 

Just to give more information, the client upgraded from SAP9 to SAP10. SAP9 the service ran 100%, but not on SAP10. 

We also have created multiple SAP10 services where it connects to SAP10 perfectly. Do you think it can be SAP user of installation failure? 

Kind regards,
Nicole Lombard.

entry.title: What's new in SAP Asset Performance Management 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to our latest release blog for SAP Asset Performance Management 2402*. This update is centred around enhancing user experience, providing more accessible data, streamlining your navigation experience and taking another step closer to closing the gap between asset strategy and execution. We've introduced new user feedback tools to gather your valuable insights, revised our naming conventions and application structures to make them more intuitive, and significantly improved our data aggregation intervals for optimal asset analysis. This update also provides enhancements for navigating easily between EAM and APM systems. Let's take a closer look at the updates.

*for simplicity I will refer in the following blog to SAP Asset Performance Management as APM. The blog topics are structured by APM business capability.

Asset Data and Information Management 

Display risk and criticality assessments and recommendations in technical object application of SAP GUI for S/4HANA 2020, SAP ERP 6.0 EHP7 & EHP8  

We have made another step closer to closing the loop between maintenance strategy and execution. 

There is a new APM tab within the EAM technical object application (SAP GUI) which provides an overview of risk and criticality assessments and recommendations for chosen technical object. From inside the EAM application you can navigate directly to the corresponding entity in the SAP Asset Performance Management system. This update makes it easier and more seamless for customers to navigate the system, providing them with all the information they need about their equipment in one place. Watch this video: 

Asset Health Monitoring

Enhanced time series data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

In the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) time series data from IoT sources is now aggregated every two minutes, as opposed to hourly, tapping into the most granular IoT insights available. This allows us to more effectively identify asset changes and predict their timing accurately, which is invaluable in optimizing maintenance planning. This more granular aggregation will significantly improve the precision of time series data within our SAC system.

Enhancements to variant management in Indicators and Indicator Monitoring apps

It is now possible to make your variants for the Indicators and Indicator Monitoring apps public.

  • This saves time because users won't need to create the same variant over and over again if someone else has already created it. Instead, they can simply use the public variant.
  • Public variants ensure consistency across the team or organization when reviewing and analyzing data. Everyone will view the data through the same lens, which can lead to more accurate analysis and conclusions.
  • It allows for standardization so that everyone on your team or in your organization can view data in the same way, leading to shared understanding and less confusion.

This feature should be used wisely to avoid the flooding of public variants. To ensure the correct ones are visible, you can mark them as favorites. Watch this quick video.

 

Display unit of measure in app View Technical Objects (Inspections)

In the newest update to the View Technical Object (Inspections) application, users can now view not only the latest indicator values, but also their respective units of measure. Previously, while indicator values were displayed, their units of measure (such as hours or millimeters) were not visible. Now all units of measure are clearly displayed next to their corresponding values in the user interface, enhancing clarity and usability across all three tabs in the application. 

Asset Reliability Engineering

Assign indicator of type Counter for a technical object

We've made a significant enhancement to the proactive type performance-based recommendations by integrating a counter-based indicator assignment (indicators where the measuring point 'Is counter' is enabled). These counters, similar to odometers in vehicles, aggregate all readings from inception to the present time, providing a comprehensive overview of an asset's usage.

These counters play a crucial role in implementing performance-based recommendations, enabling a superior level of precision and facilitating optimal asset maintenance.

To accommodate this, we've revamped the user interface for the indicators and the maintenance plan assignment screen in recommendations. It now offers distinctly sectioned views for 'assigned' and 'unassigned' indicators (counters) and maintenance plans, making it intuitive and efficient for managing such assignments.

For more information, see the SAP Help Portal Implementing a Proactive Type Performance Recommendation.

Additional filters in Manage Recommendations and Implement Recommendations applications

We've enhanced the list views or the Manage Recommendations and Implement Recommendations applications with three dynamic new filters:

  • Class: Find recommendations associated with certain classes  
  • Maintenance Plans: Easily find recommendations linked with existing maintenance plans. This filter shows only those items already associated with a maintenance planner task list. 
  • Task List: View recommendations associated with particular task lists. Recommendations linked with pre-existing task lists will appear in the filtered results. 

Extended short descriptions in Manage Recommendations app

We've made some useful usability improvements by increasing the short description of the recommendations to 256 characters in the Manage Recommendations app.

This is beneficial because:

  • Better descriptions = better insights: Enjoy the flexibility to describe your recommendation more thoroughly. 
  • Enhanced communication: The extended short description ensures that everyone involved has a clearer understanding of the recommendation
  • Adapt to growing needs: As your projects evolve, so can your assessments. Extended short descriptions ensure you're ready to handle increased complexity.

General Updates

Your opinion matters! New feedback tools in SAP Asset Performance Management

We've integrated SAP Product Experience (PX) Enablement into APM. This means you'll see a new feedback button for user-initiated surveys that is available at all times. We've also implemented time-controlled surveys. These updates serve to collect quantitative and qualitative feedback, allowing us to gain insights into user experiences, identify areas for improvement, and ultimately enhance user satisfaction. All new features are designed to be non-intrusive and user-friendly to optimally fit into your workflow.

Renaming and harmonization of application names

In 2402 we have harmonised the naming of our applications across SAP Asset Performance Management in line with SAP Fiori Guidelines to improve the product cohesion and better understand the purpose of the applications, for example, all CRUD apps (apps for creating, updating or deleting objects) now begin with the verb Manage. 

Restructuring of SAP Fiori Launchpad by business capability

In 2402 we've adapted the default display of the applications so they are structured by business capability. This revised design eases navigation by end users, helping them find applications suitable for their business needs, and create a more cohesive user experience.

Be part of the new Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) for Business AI in SAP Asset Performance Management – Register now! 

We're excited to invite you to be a part of the new SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) for Business AI in SAP Asset Performance Management (APM). This is your chance to help influence our product development and shape the future of APM. 

By participating in this initiative, you will gain valuable early insights into our latest AI feature developments. You'll have the opportunity to work directly with our product engineering teams and see first-hand how we’re innovating solutions in areas such as visual inspection, anomaly detection and assessments for maintenance strategy using AI-generated recommendations. 

Interested? Make sure to register here by March 09, 2024.  The series of virtual workshops will kick off on April 17, 2024. 

 

Keep in touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch:

  • Check out all forthcoming webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter.
  • Watch the replay of our recent webcasts
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

entry.title: Re: How to work with CAP CDS > 7 ? .How do i access and m...
entry.content:
Hi @michaelschmid03  , 

 I have come across a similar kind of situation where in additional filters were required when calling S4 odata service.

For example let say we want to filter certain dataset on CompanyCode="XYZ" , you could manipulate the req.query object in this fashion 

srv.on('READ','Myentity',async req=>{
      const someRMtservice = await cds.connect.to('SomeRemoteService')

    if(req.query.SELECT !== undefined){
      const condtionLiteral = `CompanyCode='XYZ'`

     //instantiate the cxn parser object
      const parser= cds.parse;
      // parse cxn : resultant =>   {xpr:[ {ref:['CompanyCode']}, '=', {val:'XYZ'} ]}
      const cxnObject = parser.expr(condtionLiteral);
      const cxnArray = [...cxnObject];
      req.query.SELECT.where = cxnArray;
    }

    const data = await someRMtservice.run(req.query);
    return data 
})

Please also have a look at this documentation of CXN - CXN-Expression Notation  .

Let me know if this works for you.

Thank you 

Regards

Subramaniyam N

entry.title: Re: Customized report that will show the invoiced amount,...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can try this for basic details and modify as per your requirements.

Select ORDR."DocNum" As "Order#", ORDR."CardName" , RDR1."ItemCode" , RDR1."Dscription" , RDR1."Quantity" As "Qty Ordered" ,
ODLN."DocNum" As "Deliver#", DLN1."Quantity" As "Qty Delivered" ,
ORDN."DocEntry" As "Return#", RDN1."Quantity" As "Qty Returned Before Billing" ,
OINV."DocNum" As "Invoice#", INV1."Quantity" As "Qty Billed" , RIN1."Quantity" As "Qty Credited"
From ORDR Inner Join RDR1 On ORDR."DocEntry" = RDR1."DocEntry"
Left Join DLN1 On RDR1."DocEntry" = DLN1."BaseEntry" And RDR1."LineNum" = DLN1."BaseLine"
Left Join ODLN On DLN1."DocEntry" = ODLN."DocEntry"
Left Join RDN1 On DLN1."DocEntry" = RDN1."BaseEntry" And DLN1."LineNum" = RDN1."BaseLine"
Left Join ORDN on RDN1."DocEntry" = ORDN."DocEntry"
Left Join INV1 On DLN1."DocEntry" = INV1."BaseEntry" And DLN1."LineNum" = INV1."BaseLine"
Left Join OINV On INV1."DocEntry" = OINV."DocEntry"
Left Join RIN1 On INV1."DocEntry" = RIN1."BaseEntry" And INV1."LineNum" = RIN1."BaseLine"
Left Join ORIN On RIN1."DocEntry" = ORIN."DocEntry"

entry.title: Incorrect SSL connection configuration
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a problem regarding my new SSL connection. I configured SSL between ADS and ABAP SAP. It works fine between them as long as ABAP SAP is the client and ADS the server (with the private key). The other way around is where the problem lies.

The connection should work by letting the ADS log into the SAP ABAP instance by username and password. At least that's what I tried to configure. But the test is negative.

During the error analysis with the SEC_TRACE_ANALYZER report, I noticed that the ADS tries to log in with a client certificate when attempting to use SSL. It is not clear to me why.

One idea was that I had created the destination template SecureConfigPort_Document incorrectly. I had initially created this with the X.509 certificate authentication method and continued with the SSL configuration. The authentication method has since been changed in the template. However, it is maybe possible that the connection used the template and saved the (incorrect) template somewhere. But I have not been able to determine where the template might have been used.

DO you know who I can switch the authentication method to user & password when it is configured as such but not used?

Thank you

entry.title: How to Choose the Right IT Contact for Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a powerful and innovative solution that can help you transform your business and achieve your goals. However, to ensure a smooth and successful implementation, you need to have the right IT contact on board from the start. This is the person who will be responsible for setting up the initial system access, provisioning users, and managing authentication. Alongside the Communication contacts this person will also be the recipient of all ongoing SAP communications and notifications, so they need to be available and responsive throughout the project.

 

Unfortunately, some customers make the mistake of choosing an IT contact who is not suitable for this role. For example, they might name a high-level executive, such as the CFO, who is too busy or not technically savvy enough to handle the system administration tasks. Or they might name someone who is not easily reachable or who has other priorities that prevent them from dedicating enough time and attention to the project. This can lead to serious delays and complications that can jeopardize the project timeline and quality.

 

To avoid these issues, here are some tips on how to choose the right IT contact for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project:

  • Choose someone who is a hands-on system administrator, not an executive. This person should have the skills and experience to perform the system setup and configuration tasks, as well as troubleshoot any issues that might arise.
  • Choose someone who is available and accessible throughout the project. They should also be able to respond to SAP communications and notifications promptly and effectively.
  • Choose someone who is committed and engaged with the project. This person should be willing to collaborate with the project team, provide feedback, and participate in testing and training activities. They should also be able to communicate with the business stakeholders and align the system requirements with the business goals.

 

By choosing the right IT contact for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project, you can ensure a faster and smoother implementation, and avoid unnecessary delays and complications.

entry.title: Re: Adobe form output using Gateway Services [OData] : SAP
entry.content:
@abo 

I will work backwards from (3). 

I am working on HANA 105, some of this is different before or after that release. There is an oddity with the service assignment, in that it should be maintained with Maintain Form Templates. However, your service will not appear in the dropdown of available services until it is assigned as a service on a form template. This is a paradox, as it needs to be assigned before it can be assigned, but there is a workaround.  You can make this change in transaction SFP - in fact this is the only use of SFP that you should have.

The method GET_FDP_PARAMETERS reads the above configuration, hence no need for code changes. If you do not change this, the standard delivered & assigned service is called.

Regarding the redefinition, I followed this official guide: Redefining OData Service (GW) - SAP Documentation.

Note that there are three redefinition cases. It is not too complex if you are adding fields to a DDIC based entity. In my case the model was not DDIC based for the pertinent entity and that was really difficult, as the "designers" have not provided proper inheritance in the framework, and in some cases they mess with the model in the MPC define method. The latter proved to be a problem for a colleague of mine working on billing, I helped him to fix it. Be prepared to have to replicate some of the superclass base code, either to get some methods properly redefined or mending the broken model that billing redefinition produces.  

Master pages: the simplest option would be to have two form templates which have the same content form but different masters. However, this isn't always easy to configure with the options provided by OPD but that is the theory.  You may be able to implement the form as I mentioned at the end of this post: Solved: Issue while referring the Master Page from a Maste... - SAP Community
